common.siteDescription: Open-source Agent Operating System — an AI OS with 30 LLM providers, persistent 5-tier memory, 60+ tools, multi-user collaboration with teams and API tokens, instance federation, a full web UI, and layered Parallax security. Self-hosted, powered by Deno. Apache 2.0 licensed.

nav.gettingStartedDesc: Install and run your first agent

footer.newsletterDesc: Get updates on new releases, plugins, and features.

home.heroDescription: CortexPrism is an open-source, self-hosted Agent Operating System that turns any LLM into a capable autonomous agent. This AI OS features 30 LLM providers, persistent 5-tier memory, 60+ built-in tools, sandboxed code execution, multi-user collaboration with teams and API tokens, instance federation, a full-featured web UI, and enterprise-grade Parallax security — powered by Deno. Deploy your own Agent Operating System in one command.
home.featureSectionDesc: A comprehensive Agent Operating System toolset for building, deploying, and managing AI agents at any scale.
home.ctaDescription: Works on Linux, macOS, Windows, and WSL. Ships as a single Deno binary — no Docker required to get started.

features.interactiveChatDesc: Chat with 30 LLM providers including OpenAI, Anthropic, Google, Groq, DeepSeek, and more.
features.toolUseDesc: Agents can use tools with configurable approval gates for safety.
features.memory5TierDesc: Episodic, semantic, reflection, graph, and skills tiers with hybrid FTS5 + vector retrieval.
features.parallaxSecurityDesc: Defense-in-depth with vault, policy engine, and approval workflows.
features.codeSandboxDesc: Secure code execution in sandboxed environments (Python, WASM, more).
features.modelRouterDesc: Cascade and threshold model routing for intelligent provider selection and failover.
features.daemonJobsDesc: Persistent daemon mode with scheduled jobs and background processing.
features.pluginSystemDesc: Extensible ESM/MCP/WASM plugin architecture with marketplace.
features.gitWorkspaceDesc: Full git porcelain with agent-powered commits, diffs, and branch management.
features.modelQuartermasterDesc: Adaptive 6-signal model selection engine with reinforcement learning.
features.workflowEngineDesc: DSL-based workflows with steps, branching, parallel execution, and approval gates.
features.cortexLensDesc: Immutable event log tracking every LLM call, tool execution, and policy decision.
features.selfLearningDesc: Auto-extracts reusable patterns from tool calls with 6-state lifecycle and trust tiering.
features.codeIntelligenceDesc: Tree-sitter WASM code graph across 14+ languages with call-graph traversal and impact analysis.
features.voicePipelineDesc: Speech-to-text, text-to-speech, energy-based VAD, and real-time audio streaming over WebSocket.
features.computerUseDesc: GUI automation via virtual displays with mouse, keyboard, and screenshot actions.
features.multiAgentOrchDesc: 6 orchestration strategies (sequential, parallel, debate, review-loop, hierarchical, graph DAG) for complex agent workflows.
features.swarmOrchestrationDesc: A2A-based distributed node clustering with coordinator, node registry, heartbeat monitoring, and resource snapshots.

docs.knowledgeBaseDesc: Guides, best practices, troubleshooting, and reference materials for CortexPrism.

seo.defaultTitle: CortexPrism — Open-Source Agent Operating System | AI OS for Autonomous Agents
seo.defaultDescription: CortexPrism is the open-source Agent Operating System — an AI OS with 30 LLM providers, 5-tier memory, MCP plugin marketplace, sandboxed code execution, multi-user collaboration with teams, API tokens, and federation, and enterprise-grade Parallax security. Self-host your autonomous AI agents. Apache 2.0 licensed.

about.title: About
about.headline: About CortexPrism
about.subtitle: The open-source Agent Operating System — a self-hosted AI OS, privacy-first, and powered by Deno 2.x.
about.whatIsP1: CortexPrism is an AI OS — a single-process AI Agent Operating System written in TypeScript and running on Deno 2.x. It exposes a CLI, a REST API + WebSocket server, and a full-featured web UI. All state is persisted in SQLite databases using WAL mode — no external database required.
about.whatIsP2: At its core, CortexPrism is an agent loop that orchestrates LLM calls, tool execution, memory operations, and reflection. It supports 30 LLM providers through a unified interface, with intelligent model routing via cascade and threshold strategies, plus a self-learning Model Quartermaster that predicts the optimal model for each task.
about.whatIsP3: The system features a 5-tier memory architecture with hybrid FTS5 keyword + vector embedding retrieval, a self-learning skills system that auto-extracts reusable patterns, a code intelligence engine with tree-sitter WASM parsing across 14+ languages, voice interaction pipeline, GUI automation via virtual displays, browser automation, a defense-in-depth security model (Parallax + LLM supervisor) with encrypted credential vault and DLP guard, sandboxed code execution, a git workspace with agent-powered commits, a DSL-based workflow engine, multi-user collaboration with teams and API tokens, instance federation, distributed swarm orchestration — and it all ships as a single binary.
about.whyDenoDesc: Deno provides a secure-by-default runtime with TypeScript support out of the box, modern JavaScript APIs, and efficient process management. It allows CortexPrism to run as a single binary with minimal dependencies.
about.whyOpenSourceDesc: We believe AI infrastructure should be transparent, auditable, and community-driven. The Apache 2.0 license ensures CortexPrism remains free for everyone — individuals, startups, and enterprises alike.
about.providerFreedomDesc: No vendor lock-in. Use any LLM provider — or multiple at once. Switch between Anthropic, OpenAI, Google, local Ollama models, or any of 30 providers with a single flag.
about.extensibleDesc: Built-in tool system, plugin architecture supporting ESM/MCP/WASM, and a full REST API. Extend CortexPrism with custom tools, plugins, and integrations.
about.principlePrivacyDesc: All data stored locally. No telemetry, no tracking. Your conversations, memory, and credentials remain on your machine.
about.principleSecurityDesc: Defense-in-depth with Parallax security model. Every tool call validated, every access logged, credentials encrypted.
about.principleProviderDesc: Use any LLM provider. Switch freely. The unified interface means your workflows are portable.
about.principleLocalDesc: Run entirely on your machine. No cloud dependency for core functionality. Optional connectivity for LLM APIs.
about.principleCommunityDesc: Apache 2.0 licensed. Contributions welcome. The roadmap is shaped by the community.
about.builtWithLove: Built with love for the AI community

useCases.subtitle: CortexPrism is not a single-purpose tool — it is an open-source Agent Operating System that adapts to your workflow. From personal AI companions to enterprise-grade secure deployments, explore how teams and individuals are putting autonomous agents to work.
useCases.ctaHeadline: Ready to put the Agent Operating System to work?
useCases.ctaDesc: Install CortexPrism in one command and start building your first agent workflow in under a minute. Fully open source. Apache 2.0.
useCases.personalAISub: Your own intelligent assistant with persistent memory
useCases.personalAIDesc: Replace generic AI chat interfaces with a personalized assistant that remembers your context, preferences, and project details across sessions. CortexPrism runs entirely on your machine — your data never leaves your infrastructure.
useCases.personalAIAudience: Developers, researchers, and power users who want an AI companion that actually remembers them.
useCases.personalAIH1: Persistent memory across sessions — never re-explain context
useCases.personalAIH2: Multi-provider support — use the best model for each task
useCases.personalAIH3: Tool integration — files, web search, code execution
useCases.personalAIH4: Sandboxed execution for safe data analysis and automation
useCases.researchDesc: Accelerate research with web search, code execution, and persistent knowledge storage. CortexPrism becomes your personal research lab — gather data, run analysis, and build a searchable knowledge base that grows with every session.
useCases.researchAudience: Analysts, academics, journalists, and anyone doing deep-dive research on complex topics.
useCases.researchH1: Real-time web search via DuckDuckGo integration
useCases.researchH2: Python sandbox for data analysis, visualization, and statistics
useCases.researchH3: Semantic memory for building a personal knowledge graph
useCases.researchH4: Full session history for revisiting and continuing past research
useCases.devDesc: An AI pair programmer that reads your codebase, executes and debugs code in sandboxed environments, and remembers your project context across sessions. Think of it as an always-on senior engineer who knows your entire codebase.
useCases.devAudience: Software engineers who want AI-assisted coding without sending their code to third-party cloud services.
useCases.devH1: File introspection for deep codebase understanding
useCases.devH2: Multi-language sandboxed execution with resource limits
useCases.devH3: Auto-fix loop for automated debugging and remediation
useCases.devH4: Shell command execution with configurable approval gates
useCases.cicdDesc: Set up automated workflows with CRON-based scheduling, daemon background processing, and policy-controlled automation. Monitor systems, generate recurring reports, and automate routine maintenance — all with full audit trails.
useCases.cicdAudience: DevOps engineers, SREs, and teams looking to automate operational workflows with AI oversight.
useCases.cicdH1: CRON-based job scheduling for recurring tasks
useCases.cicdH2: Daemon mode for persistent background agent processing
useCases.cicdH3: Policy engine for safe, auditable automation at scale
useCases.cicdH4: Full audit trail via Cortex Lens for compliance
useCases.knowledgeSub: Build and query a personal knowledge base
useCases.knowledgeDesc: Leverage the 5-tier memory system — episodic, semantic, reflection, graph, and skills — to store, retrieve, and consolidate information. Build a second brain that evolves with every interaction and surfaces insights you didn't know you had.
useCases.knowledgeAudience: Knowledge workers, team leads, and anyone managing complex, evolving information domains.
useCases.knowledgeH1: 5-tier memory with hybrid FTS5 + vector similarity search
useCases.knowledgeH2: Episodic memory of past conversations with auto-decay scoring
useCases.knowledgeH3: Semantic memory for facts, concepts, and relationships
useCases.knowledgeH4: Weekly reflection patterns for meta-learning and knowledge consolidation
useCases.secureDesc: Deploy AI agents with confidence using the Parallax security model: a 3-stage tool validation gate, LLM security supervisor, AES-256-GCM encrypted credential vault, DLP Guard with 22 scanners, and granular policy controls. Built for organizations that cannot compromise on security.
useCases.secureAudience: Security teams, enterprises, and regulated industries deploying AI agents in production environments.
useCases.secureH1: 3-stage validation gate + LLM security supervisor for every tool call
useCases.secureH2: AES-256-GCM encrypted credential vault with PBKDF2 key derivation
useCases.secureH3: DLP Guard with 22 content scanners for data loss prevention
useCases.secureH4: Comprehensive, immutable audit logging via Cortex Lens
useCases.voiceDesc: Interact with your AI agent using natural speech with a full voice pipeline: speech-to-text via OpenAI Whisper, text-to-speech with OpenAI TTS or ElevenLabs, and energy-based voice activity detection for seamless turn-taking conversation.
useCases.voiceAudience: Users who want a conversational AI experience, accessibility applications, and hands-free workflows.
useCases.voiceH1: Speech-to-text via OpenAI Whisper for natural voice input
useCases.voiceH2: Text-to-speech with OpenAI TTS or ElevenLabs voices
useCases.voiceH3: Energy-based VAD for automatic turn detection
useCases.voiceH4: Real-time audio streaming over WebSocket for low-latency interaction
useCases.guiDesc: Automate GUI applications, fill forms, scrape visual data, and control desktop environments with precision mouse and keyboard control. Perfect for legacy system integration, RPA workflows, and testing visual interfaces.
useCases.guiAudience: QA engineers, RPA developers, and teams integrating with legacy systems that lack APIs.
useCases.guiH1: Virtual display automation via Xvfb for headless environments
useCases.guiH2: Precision mouse and keyboard control with coordinate accuracy
useCases.guiH3: Screenshot capture with visual analysis capabilities
useCases.guiH4: Docker-isolated or native desktop runtime for flexible deployment
useCases.codebaseDesc: Index your entire codebase with tree-sitter WASM parsing across 14+ languages. Navigate call graphs, trace execution paths, visualize dependencies with D3.js, and analyze the impact of changes before you make them.
useCases.codebaseAudience: Software architects, tech leads, and developers onboarding to large or legacy codebases.
useCases.codebaseH1: Multi-language parsing across 14+ languages including TS, Python, Go, Rust, Java
useCases.codebaseH2: Cross-file call graph resolution with 6-strategy target resolution
useCases.codebaseH3: Interactive dependency graphs via D3.js in the Web UI
useCases.codebaseH4: Impact analysis and path tracing for change management

featuresDetail.heading: Agent Operating System Features
featuresDetail.subtitle: Everything you need to build, deploy, and manage AI agent applications — all in one open-source Agent Operating System.
featuresDetail.interactiveChatDesc: Chat with 30 LLM providers through a unified interface. Switch between OpenAI, Anthropic Claude, Google Gemini, Groq, DeepSeek, and more without changing your workflow.
featuresDetail.interactiveChatB1: One interface for all major LLM providers
featuresDetail.interactiveChatB3: Streaming responses with real-time token display
featuresDetail.interactiveChatB4: Conversation history with search and export
featuresDetail.toolUseApprovalDesc: Agents can use tools and call functions, with configurable approval gates that let you review and approve or reject tool calls before they execute.
featuresDetail.toolUseApprovalB1: Built-in tool system for code execution, file I/O, web access
featuresDetail.toolUseApprovalB4: Full audit log of all tool calls
featuresDetail.memory5TierDesc: A layered memory architecture with five tiers: episodic (turn summaries with auto-decay), semantic (injected facts with hybrid FTS5 + vector search), reflection (LLM-extracted behavior patterns), graph (knowledge graph entities and relationships), and skills (procedural knowledge from sessions).
featuresDetail.memory5TierB1: Episodic: Turn summaries with configurable half-life and auto-decay scoring
featuresDetail.memory5TierB2: Semantic: Injected facts with hybrid FTS5 + cosine vector similarity search
featuresDetail.memory5TierB3: Reflection: LLM-extracted behavioral patterns with weekly consolidation
featuresDetail.memory5TierB4: Graph: Knowledge graph nodes and edges built from semantic entity extraction
featuresDetail.memory5TierB5: Skills: Procedural knowledge extracted from sessions with multi-step tool usage
featuresDetail.parallaxSecurityDesc: A multi-layered security architecture with encrypted vault storage, granular policy engine, role-based access control, and approval workflows.
featuresDetail.parallaxSecurityB1: Encrypted vault for secrets and credentials
featuresDetail.codeSandboxDesc: Execute Python, JavaScript, Wasm, and shell commands in isolated sandboxes. Perfect for data analysis, automation, and prototyping.
featuresDetail.codeSandboxB1: Python sandbox with pip package support
featuresDetail.modelRouterDesc: Two intelligent routing strategies wrapping LLM providers. Cascade tries cheapest providers first and escalates on low confidence. Threshold scores prompt complexity to route between strong and weak models.
featuresDetail.modelRouterB1: Cascade router: cheapest model first, escalate on low confidence
featuresDetail.modelRouterB2: Threshold router: RouteLLM-style complexity scoring for prompt routing
featuresDetail.modelRouterB3: Cost optimization with confidence-based fallback between providers
featuresDetail.daemonJobsDesc: Persistent daemon mode with a built-in job scheduler. Run agents in the background, schedule recurring tasks, and monitor execution.
featuresDetail.pluginSystemDesc: Extend CortexPrism with a powerful plugin system supporting ESM modules, Model Context Protocol servers, and WebAssembly plugins with supply-chain verification.
featuresDetail.pluginSystemB1: Three plugin types: ESM, MCP, WASM
featuresDetail.webUiDesc: Built-in web interface and comprehensive REST API for managing agents, monitoring sessions, and configuring the system remotely.
featuresDetail.agentManagerDesc: 10 built-in specialized agents (Assistant, Developer, Researcher, Architect, Analyst, Writer, DevOps, Security, Code Reviewer, QA) plus custom agent profiles with different personalities, tools, and provider configurations.
featuresDetail.microServicesDesc: Run CortexPrism as a distributed system with micro-service architecture. Scale components independently and deploy across multiple machines.
featuresDetail.gitWorkspaceDesc: Full git porcelain interface powered by the Cortex agent. Auto-generated commit messages, diff analysis, branch management, and PR-ready workflows.
featuresDetail.gitWorkspaceB1: Ten git subcommands: status, log, diff, add, commit, push, pull, clone, branch, remote
featuresDetail.gitWorkspaceB2: Agent-generated conventional commit messages from diffs
featuresDetail.gitWorkspaceB3: Diff analysis with natural language summaries
featuresDetail.gitWorkspaceB4: Intelligent branch naming and conflict detection
featuresDetail.modelQuartermasterDesc: An adaptive 6-signal model-selection engine that observes LLM calls, learns patterns, and predicts the optimal model for each task. Powered by reinforcement learning.
featuresDetail.modelQuartermasterB1: Six prediction signals: trajectory, episodic, historical, cost, quality, reflection
featuresDetail.modelQuartermasterB2: Automatic tool execution above 90% confidence for safe operations
featuresDetail.modelQuartermasterB3: Reinforcement learning with EMA weight adjustment (alpha=0.15 reward, alpha=0.25 punishment)
featuresDetail.modelQuartermasterB4: Dashboard with accuracy bars, signal weights, and top tools
featuresDetail.workflowEngineDesc: A DSL-based workflow engine for defining and executing multi-step agent tasks. Supports sequential steps, conditional branching, parallel execution, and human approval gates.
featuresDetail.workflowEngineB1: Five node types: step, branch, parallel, goto, wait
featuresDetail.workflowEngineB4: Human-in-the-loop approval gates with resume support
featuresDetail.cortexLensDesc: A comprehensive audit and telemetry event log that records every action in the system — LLM calls, tool execution, security decisions, memory operations, and quartermaster predictions.
featuresDetail.cortexLensB1: 50+ event types tracked with session, actor, and time indexing
featuresDetail.cortexLensB2: Immutable event log stored in dedicated lens.db SQLite database
featuresDetail.cortexLensB3: Web UI activity dashboard with timeline view
featuresDetail.cortexLensB4: REST API for programmatic audit log access
featuresDetail.skillsSystemDesc: Auto-extracts reusable procedural patterns from successful tool-call sequences and stores them as versioned, quality-scored skills with trust tiering and dependency tracking.
featuresDetail.skillsSystemB1: 6-state lifecycle: candidate → verified → released → degraded → deprecated → archived
featuresDetail.skillsSystemB2: Embedding-based retrieval with cosine similarity matching to user queries
featuresDetail.skillsSystemB3: Automatic deduplication and merging of similar skills
featuresDetail.skillsSystemB4: 4-tier trust system controlling agent skill exposure
featuresDetail.codeIntelligenceDesc: Tree-sitter WASM parser for 14+ languages with call graph resolution, impact analysis, architecture extraction, and symbol search. Visualized via D3.js force-directed graph.
featuresDetail.codeIntelligenceB1: 14+ languages: TypeScript, JavaScript, Python, Go, Rust, Java, Kotlin, C, C++, Ruby, PHP, Swift, Lua, Bash
featuresDetail.codeIntelligenceB2: 6-strategy call target resolution with cross-file import analysis
featuresDetail.codeIntelligenceB3: 12 node labels and 18 edge types in the graph database
featuresDetail.codeIntelligenceB4: D3.js Web UI with symbol search, impact analysis, and path tracing
featuresDetail.voicePipelineDesc: Full voice interaction pipeline with OpenAI Whisper STT, OpenAI TTS / ElevenLabs, energy-based VAD, and real-time audio streaming over WebSocket.
featuresDetail.voicePipelineB1: Speech-to-text via OpenAI Whisper with configurable models
featuresDetail.voicePipelineB2: Text-to-speech with OpenAI TTS and ElevenLabs provider support
featuresDetail.voicePipelineB3: Energy-based voice activity detection with adjustable threshold
featuresDetail.voicePipelineB4: Real-time audio streaming over WebSocket for low-latency interaction
featuresDetail.computerUseDesc: GUI automation using X11 virtual framebuffer (Xvfb) with mouse control, keyboard input, and screenshot capture. Docker-isolated or native runtime.
featuresDetail.computerUseB1: Virtual display management with multi-display support
featuresDetail.computerUseB2: Mouse: coordinate movement, left/right/middle clicks, double/triple clicks, drag
featuresDetail.computerUseB3: Keyboard: text typing with configurable delays, key combos, key holding
featuresDetail.computerUseB4: 15 agent actions: screenshot, click, type, key, scroll, wait, mouse_move, drag
featuresDetail.browserAutomationDesc: Headless Playwright-powered browser for web navigation, interaction, screenshots, and accessibility snapshots. Fully integrated with agent tools.
featuresDetail.browserAutomationB1: Navigate to URLs, click elements, type text, and submit forms
featuresDetail.browserAutomationB2: Take full-page screenshots and accessibility snapshots
featuresDetail.browserAutomationB3: Execute JavaScript on pages and wait for conditions
featuresDetail.browserAutomationB4: All actions gated through policy validator with user approval
featuresDetail.multiUserDesc: Multi-user platform with PBKDF2 password hashing, team management with join policies, scoped API tokens, and cross-user resource sharing. Instance admin controls with user enable/disable.
featuresDetail.multiUserB1: User accounts with PBKDF2 password hashing
featuresDetail.multiUserB2: Teams with admin/member roles and join policies (open, invite, closed)
featuresDetail.multiUserB3: Scoped API tokens with expiration and last-used tracking
featuresDetail.multiUserB4: Resource sharing between users with ownership validation
featuresDetail.instanceFederationDesc: Establish trust relationships between CortexPrism instances via pairing tokens. Federated instances can share resources, coordinate agents, and exchange messages securely.
featuresDetail.instanceFederationB1: Pairing token generation for secure trust establishment
featuresDetail.instanceFederationB5: Revocable trust with one-click peer removal
featuresDetail.a2aProtocolDesc: Google Agent2Agent (A2A) v1.0 protocol for cross-framework agent collaboration. JSON-RPC 2.0 server/client with SSE streaming and tool wrapping.
featuresDetail.a2aProtocolB1: Full A2A v1.0 compliance with JSON-RPC 2.0 transport
featuresDetail.a2aProtocolB2: Server mode: expose CortexPrism agents to other A2A-compatible frameworks
featuresDetail.a2aProtocolB3: Client mode: consume external A2A agents as CortexPrism tools
featuresDetail.a2aProtocolB4: SSE streaming for real-time agent-to-agent communication

installPage.heading: Install CortexPrism
installPage.subtitle: One command to get started on Linux, macOS, or Windows.
installPage.manualInstallDesc: Clone the repo directly and run with Deno:
installPage.precompiledDesc: Download the latest binary from the GitHub Releases page. All binaries include SHA-256 checksums and optional GPG signatures.
installPage.postInstallDesc: After installing, run these commands to get started:

securityPage.heading: Security Architecture
securityPage.subtitle: Defense in depth through multiple independent security layers. Every operation is validated, logged, and controlled.
securityPage.defaultDenyDesc: The following dangerous patterns are blocked by default on first migration:
securityPage.vaultDescription: All sensitive credentials are encrypted using AES-256-GCM with PBKDF2 key derivation (200,000 iterations, SHA-256). The passphrase is never stored — only held in the environment variable at runtime.
securityPage.vaultDetail1: AES-256-GCM encryption for all stored secrets
securityPage.vaultDetail4: Passphrase held only in environment variable (CORTEX_VAULT_KEY)
securityPage.parallaxDescription: Every tool call passes through a 3-stage validation gate before execution. Any stage can deny the operation, preventing unauthorized or dangerous actions.
securityPage.parallaxDetail1: Stage 1: Tool name policy check — is this tool allowed?
securityPage.parallaxDetail2: Stage 2: Shell command pattern check — regex deny rules for dangerous commands
securityPage.parallaxDetail3: Stage 3: Domain allow/deny — for web search URLs
securityPage.parallaxDetail4: All decisions logged as policy_check events in Lens
securityPage.parallaxDetail5: Seeded deny rules for dangerous patterns (rm -rf /, fork bombs, disk writes)
securityPage.parallaxDetail6: Default-allow when no rules match — add explicit deny rules for sensitive operations
securityPage.policyDescription: Granular allow/deny rules with priority-based evaluation. Rules are stored in SQLite and evaluated by priority (lower number = higher precedence).
securityPage.policyDetail1: Six rule kinds: tool, shell, domain, capability, path, computer
securityPage.policyDetail5: Pre-seeded deny rules for dangerous shell patterns
securityPage.cplDescription: YAML-based policy files defining capability boundaries for the agent. Rules can be managed via the CLI or auto-loaded from a policy file. Each rule specifies kind, effect, pattern, and priority.
securityPage.cplDetail1: YAML policy files with versioned schema
securityPage.cplDetail2: Rules evaluated by priority order (ASC) — first match wins
securityPage.cplDetail3: Supports shell, tool, and domain rule kinds
securityPage.cplDetail4: Allow or deny effects with descriptive reasons
securityPage.sandboxDescription: Code execution happens in isolated Docker containers with strict resource limits and no network access. Subprocess fallback available when Docker is not present.
securityPage.sandboxDetail3: 64 PID limit and no-new-privileges security flag
securityPage.sandboxDetail5: 64KB output cap to prevent log flooding
securityPage.lensDescription: Every security-relevant event is logged to the Cortex Lens database, providing a complete audit trail for all system activity.
securityPage.lensDetail1: All LLM calls logged (provider, model, tokens, cost)
securityPage.lensDetail2: All tool calls logged (tool name, arguments, timestamp)
securityPage.lensDetail3: All policy checks logged (allowed/denied with reason)
securityPage.lensDetail4: All vault access logged (credential name, access time)
securityPage.approvalDescription: Sensitive operations can require explicit user approval before execution. The shell and code_exec tools have configurable approval gates that can be toggled per session or policy.
securityPage.approvalDetail1: Shell command execution can require approval (configurable)
securityPage.approvalDetail2: Code execution in sandbox can require approval (configurable)
securityPage.approvalDetail4: Policy-based automatic approval for trusted patterns
securityPage.approvalDetail5: Full audit of all approved and rejected operations
securityPage.llmSupervisorDescription: Sensitive data access (memory, databases, screenshots) requires approval from a fast LLM supervisor model with decision caching and human escalation for uncertain cases.
securityPage.llmSupervisorDetail1: Fast supervisor models: Gemini 2.0 Flash, GPT-4o Mini
securityPage.llmSupervisorDetail2: Decision caching with 1-hour session TTL to reduce latency
securityPage.llmSupervisorDetail3: Human escalation for uncertain classification cases
securityPage.llmSupervisorDetail4: Structured approval pipeline with auto-approve thresholds
securityPage.llmSupervisorDetail5: Webhook notifications for pending approvals with 5-minute timeouts
securityPage.llmSupervisorDetail6: Temporary grants: approved access cached per session to prevent approval fatigue
securityPage.dlpGuardDescription: Automatic sensitivity detection and data loss prevention. Classifies data as SECRET/SENSITIVE/NORMAL/PUBLIC with 22 DLP scanners monitoring all agent outputs.
securityPage.dlpGuardDetail1: Automatic classification: SECRET/SENSITIVE/NORMAL/PUBLIC based on pattern matching
securityPage.dlpGuardDetail2: Detects passwords, API keys, PII, PHI, PCI, and confidential markers
securityPage.dlpGuardDetail3: 22-scanner DLP system monitoring all agent outputs
securityPage.dlpGuardDetail4: Three action levels: monitor (log only), redact (mask in output), block (deny entirely)
securityPage.dlpGuardDetail5: All existing data backfilled with classifications on first run
securityPage.dlpGuardDetail6: AI Guardrails: 5 pluggable classifiers for prompt injection, PII leakage, harmful code, excessive length, shell injection

contributePage.heading: Contribute
contributePage.subtitle: Help us build the future of AI agent technology. Contributions of all kinds are welcome.
contributePage.licenseText: CortexPrism is an open-source project released under the Apache 2.0 License. We welcome contributions from the community — whether it's fixing bugs, adding features, improving documentation, or developing plugins. Every contribution makes the project better for everyone.
contributePage.devSetupDesc: To set up a local development environment:
contributePage.devSetupInstructions: For more details, check the Contributing Guide on GitHub.
contributePage.reportIssuesDesc: Found a bug or have a feature request? Open an issue on GitHub with a clear description, reproduction steps, and expected behavior.
contributePage.joinCommunityDesc: Chat with developers and users on Discord. Get help, share ideas, discuss features, and stay up to date with the latest development.
contributePage.submitPrsDesc: Fork the repository, create a feature branch, make your changes, and submit a PR. Make sure to follow the coding conventions and add tests.
contributePage.developPluginsDesc: Build plugins for the CortexPrism marketplace. Plugins can be ESM modules, MCP servers, or WebAssembly modules.

marketplaceHub.heading: Marketplace
marketplaceHub.subtitle: Discover plugins and agent configurations for the CortexPrism ecosystem.
marketplaceHub.pluginsDesc: Extend CortexPrism with powerful plugins. ESM modules, MCP servers, and WASM runtimes.
marketplaceHub.agentsDesc: Discover pre-configured agent profiles for various tasks and domains.
marketplaceHub.overviewPluginsDesc: ESM modules, MCP servers, and WASM runtimes
marketplaceHub.overviewAgentsDesc: Pre-configured agent profiles for various tasks
marketplaceHub.easyIntegrationDesc: Quickly integrate plugins and agents into your CortexPrism setup with standardized installation commands.
marketplaceHub.communityDrivenDesc: Discover and share community-built extensions that solve real-world problems and extend platform capabilities.
marketplaceHub.curatedQualityDesc: All marketplace items are reviewed for quality, security, and compatibility with the CortexPrism ecosystem.

blogList.noPostsDesc: Check back soon for new content.

marketplaceList.publishAgentDesc: Share your agent configuration with the CortexPrism community.
marketplaceList.importDesc: Automatically import a {type} by providing its public GitHub repository URL. The manifest will be read from the repository root.
marketplaceList.importUrlHelp: Public GitHub repository containing a cortex.json or manifest.json
marketplaceList.importHelp: The importer will read the manifest from the repository root, fetch GitHub metadata (stars, topics, license), and create a submission. Required manifest fields: name, version, kind (esm/mcp/wasm) for plugins, or provider/model for agents.
marketplaceList.submissionDescDynamic: Your {type} has been submitted for review. You can track its status on your dashboard.
marketplaceList.reviewNote: Submissions are reviewed by admins before being published. Track your submission status on the dashboard.
marketplaceList.fieldDescriptionPlaceholder: A brief description of your submission...
marketplaceList.capabilitiesHelp: List the capability identifiers your plugin provides
marketplaceList.systemPromptPlaceholder: You are an expert agent that...
marketplaceList.repositoryHelp: GitHub repository for auto-fetching stars, topics, and license info
marketplaceList.noReviewsDesc: Be the first to share your thoughts!
marketplaceList.pluginReviewEmpty: Be the first to rate this plugin!
marketplaceList.agentReviewEmpty: Be the first to rate this agent!
marketplaceList.pluginReviewPlaceholder: Share your experience with this plugin...
marketplaceList.agentReviewPlaceholder: Share your experience with this agent...
